channel.title: Antimatter on Dyson Sphere Program Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Antimatter on Dyson Sphere Program Guide

item.title: DSP Power Transition Guide (v0.10) - From Wind Turbines to Antimatter Without Blackouts
item.description:
Short answer: Never skip power tiers. Wind turbines handle early game (build 20-30). Thermal generators power mid game with refined oil or coal (one refinery feeds 3 generators). Fusion plants carry late game (deuterium rods last 4+ hours per plant). Antimatter is endgame only - skip it until you have a stable Dyson Sphere producing critical photons.

Power is the single most common reason DSP factories stop. A blackout cascades - miners stop, belts stop, power generators starve, more things stop. Understanding each power tier and when to transition prevents hours of troubleshooting.

item.title: 1 Artificial Sun Replaces 125 Wind Turbines - DSP Endgame Power Loop Guide
item.description:
Critical photons and antimatter are the gateway to endgame power in DSP. The loop is confusing at first: photons come from the sphere, make antimatter, make fuel rods, power your planet. Here is exactly how the chain works, step by step.

TL;DR: The Endgame Power Loop

Ray Receivers capture Dyson Sphere power and output Critical Photons. Particle Colliders turn photons into Antimatter + Hydrogen. Assemblers combine Antimatter with other materials to make Deuterium Fuel Rods or Antimatter Fuel Rods. Artificial Suns burn these rods for massive power. The loop produces more power than it consumes - but only if designed correctly.
